bids.reasoning: At t=0.0 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent opened with all valueF, which is very poor for my preferences and gives no reliable sign of concession yet, so I will counter with my strongest valid values on every issue.

At t=0.02 it is very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession to avoid simply repeating my previous all-best offer. The opponent has held firm on all valueF, which is very low utility for me, and there is no message or valid evidence yet that I should make a large concession. I am conceding slightly on issueC to valueB while holding my highest-weight issueA and other important issues at valueA.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my ideal package. The opponent has repeatedly offered mostly valueF, and their latest change to issueD:valueE is actually worse for me than issueD:valueF, so there is no evidence yet of meaningful concession toward my preferences. I will avoid repeating my last exact bid by conceding slightly on issueC while holding firm on the higher-value issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still very early, so there is little reason to make major concessions yet. The opponent has stayed near an all-valueF position and has not shown meaningful concession toward my preferences, so I will only make a small non-repeated concession on issueC while holding my higher-priority issueA and strong values on issueB/issueD.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid rather than making broad concessions. The opponent has mostly held at very low-value offers for me, with only a small signal on issueC moving to valueC, so I will keep my most important issues firm and concede only on issueC where the cost is modest.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.856, since it is still very early at t=0.10 and the opponent’s offers remain far below my preferences. The opponent has only made very small concessions, mainly on issueC/issueD, so I will concede slightly on issueC while holding firm on the most valuable combination for issueA, issueB, and issueD.

At t=0.12 this is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a limited concession on issueC, where the opponent has twice offered valueC. The opponent is still holding very unfavorable values for issueA and issueB, so I will hold firm on my most important/high-value positions rather than concede broadly.

At t=0.14 it is still early, and the opponent’s bids remain very low utility for me despite small movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD. I am targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding repeating my immediately previous offer, conceding only slightly on issueC where the utility loss is relatively small.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 since it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for my preferences, with only small movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueC: valueC, so I am conceding there while holding firm on my highest-value positions for issueA, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 since it is still early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain very low for my preferences, with only small movement on issueA/issueC/issueD and no concession on issueB. I will keep my high-weight issueA and other important issues at valueA, while making a modest concession on issueC and avoiding repeating my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on issueC, where valueC still gives me decent utility and the opponent has repeatedly offered it. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and remain low overall, especially on issueB and issueD, so I am holding firm on my higher-value positions there.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.22 and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me, with only small concessions. The opponent seems to be pushing hard on low values for issueB and issueD while sometimes moving on issueA and issueC, so I will hold firm on my most important issues and make only a limited concession on issueC while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

At t=0.24 I am still targeting a fairly high utility bid, while making a visible concession from my earlier mostly top-value offers. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and they are holding issueB near my worst values, but they have sometimes moved issueA to valueD and issueC to valueC, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and concede moderately on lower-priority tradeoff space.

At t=0.26 it is still early enough to target a strong utility for me, but I should continue making limited concessions rather than repeat my prior bid. The opponent’s bids remain very low for my utility, though they have shown interest in issueC=valueC and issueD=valueE/F, so I concede on issueC and partially on issueD while holding firm on my most important issueA and valuable issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around the low-to-mid 0.7s because it is only t=0.28, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held very low-value offers for me, with small movement on issueA and issueC, so I am conceding on issueC and issueD while holding firm on the higher-value issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.71, appropriate for t=0.30 where there is some time pressure but not enough to make large concessions. The opponent has slowly improved from all valueF toward some concessions on issueA and issueC, but they continue to push hard on issueB and issueD; I will concede on issueD and choose a mutually signaled issueC value while holding firm on my higher-priority issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility a little above 0.70, since we are still only around one-third of the way through the negotiation and the opponent’s offers remain very low for me despite small movement. The opponent has repeatedly signaled flexibility around issueC and issueD while staying poor on issueA/issueB, so I will hold firm on my most important issueA and on issueB, while conceding on issueC/issueD to values they have recently used. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, making only moderate concessions because we are still before the midpoint and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me despite some small movement. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueD as valueF/valueE and sometimes issueC as valueC/valueD, so I concede issueD to valueF while holding firm on my most important issueA and strong issueB preference. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on offer behavior.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.71, keeping my high-value positions on issueA and issueB while conceding substantially on issueD and moderately on issueC. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I should not drop too far; the opponent’s offers remain low for me and especially stubborn on issueB, though they have shown some movement on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63: still well above reservation, but with clearer concessions than my prior offers because we are now in the mid-game. The opponent has persistently pushed issueD toward valueF and issueC toward valueC/F, so I concede issueD to valueF and keep issueC at valueC while protecting my highest-weight issueA at valueA. I also soften issueB from my ideal valueA to valueC to avoid repeating my prior pattern and test whether a broader concession helps.

I am targeting about 0.71 utility, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting my highest-weight issueA. The opponent has mostly held firm on low-value choices for me, but their latest offer conceded issueB to valueA and they have repeatedly offered issueC:valueC and issueD:valueF, so I am incorporating those signals while keeping issueA at my best value.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.73 by holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueB while conceding issueD to valueF, which the opponent has repeatedly signaled. At t=0.42 there is some pressure to move toward overlap, and their latest offer included issueC: valueB, so I can accept that on issueC while still keeping a strong overall bid.

I am targeting a still-solid mid-game utility around 0.63, keeping issueA at valueA because it is my highest-weight issue while conceding on lower/equal-weight issues that the opponent has repeatedly emphasized. The opponent’s offers remain low for me but show some movement on issueB and issueC, so I am matching their recent issueB concession to valueD and keeping issueD at valueF, which they have frequently proposed. With time at 0.44, I should make a meaningful concession without dropping to their very unfavorable bids yet.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.73 because we are only mid-negotiation at t=0.46, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent has remained tough on issueA and issueD but has recently offered issueB:valueA and once offered issueC:valueB, so I keep my high-priority issueA at valueA while conceding issueD to valueF and using issueC:valueB as a plausible compromise.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.66, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still well above the opponent’s latest offer utility to me. At t=0.48 there is pressure to move toward combinations the opponent has signaled, and their latest bid shows willingness on issueA=valueB and issueC=valueB while they continue to push low values on issueD, so I concede issueD to valueF but hold issueB at my best value. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the bid history.

At t=0.50 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65, conceding from my early high-utility bids but still preserving value on the most important issueA. The opponent’s latest offer significantly improved issueA to valueA while keeping issueD at valueF, so I will accept their signal on issueD and mostly work around issueB/issueC tradeoffs rather than returning to my prior exact bid.

At t=0.52 I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65, conceding slightly while preserving my highest-weight issueA at valueA. The opponent’s best recent signal was around issueA:valueA, issueB:valueB, issueC:valueC, issueD:valueF, and they have also offered issueC:valueB before, so I am proposing a nearby tradeoff that improves issueC for me while accepting concessions on issueB and issueD.

At t=0.54 I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.628 by keeping my highest-weight issueA at valueA while conceding on issueD to valueF and using middling values on issueB/issueC. The opponent has not sent messages, but their offer at t=0.50 exactly included this combination, so it is a credible bid they may accept while still giving me a positive payoff. This also avoids repeating my immediately previous bid.
